topics: Session search and pattern extraction workflow
DeepSeek v4-pro API timeout / broken pipe failure
improvements: Reduce agent-self-improvement skill size (35K chars): The skill loads all 5 mechanisms (Dreaming, Memory Health, Skill Promotion Gate, Persistent Goals, Soul Evolution) plus full project context tables, DSPy integration, proposal queue, and user preference signals even though the oracular dream cron only needs Mechanism 1 (Dreaming). Extract a focused dreaming-only sub-skill that loads ~3-5K chars instead of 35K.

Enable thinking mode on dreaming cron: The oracular dream is a complex synthesis task (scan sessions, extract patterns, consolidate to memory/skills). Thinking mode would allow multi-step reasoning within a single API call without round-trips, reducing the chance of broken-pipe mid-stream on long prompts.

Add model fallback for dreaming cron: This session failed with deepseek-v4-pro. A fallback to deepseek-v4-flash or a smaller model (e.g., prometheus-smart) for the dreaming task would provide resilience — dreaming is consolidation work that tolerates lower capability but must not silently skip for 11+ days.

Dreaming cron should check last_dream_at and accumulate: dreaming_state.json shows last successful dream was June 15 (11 days ago). The cron prompt says 'session_search since last dream' but if every attempt fails at the API level, no state is updated. Add a pre-flight check that detects multi-day gaps and warns or escalates.

Split cron prompt into delivery instructions vs. task: 35K of the user prompt is the agent-self-improvement skill; only ~714 chars is the actual cron job prompt. The skill contains extensive material (goal judge config, proposal queue CLI commands, DSPy paths, soul evolution rollback scripts) that the dreaming task never uses. A slimmed skill loading only the dreaming mechanism would cut tokens by ~80%.
pain_points: Devyn's oracular dream has not completed successfully since June 15 — 11 days of silent failures. dreaming_state.json is frozen at 2026-06-15. No sessions have been consolidated into memory or skills for nearly two weeks.

Broken pipe (errno 32) on deepseek-v4-pro: The API connection is severed mid-stream before any response is received. This is a network-level failure, not a rate limit or server error. The ~62K char prompt may be hitting connection timeout thresholds.

Only one session dump exists for Devyn (aside from a Jun 3 file), despite eight configured cron jobs. The oracular dream failure dumps are not accumulating (possibly being overwritten), making failure pattern analysis harder.

The agent-self-improvement skill loads massive content irrelevant to dreaming: DSPy integration paths, soul evolution rollback scripts, /goal slash command tables, proposal queue Python commands, multi-agent profile descriptions, and Repetition Detection rules that only apply during live chat — none of which are used by the nightly dreaming cron.

No retry response exists in the dump: reason is 'max_retries_exhausted' but the retries array is empty/absent. This suggests the retry mechanism itself may not be capturing intermediate attempts or all attempts failed at the transport layer before any HTTP response.
compliments: The iris-oracular-dream cron is well-conceived: nightly session consolidation with session_search → extract patterns → consolidate to memory/skills → update dreaming_state.json is exactly the right automation loop for agent self-improvement.

The cron job prompt is clean and well-structured: clear numbered steps (1-5), explicit tool names (session_search, honcho_conclude, memory, skill_manage), and silent-exit behavior for when there's nothing new to consolidate.
The dreaming_state.json schema is well-designed: tracks last_dream_at, sessions_processed, honcho_conclusions_saved, and version, making it auditable and resumable.

All other Devyn cron jobs (ambient collect, ambient synthesis, skill trace optimizer, post-session, weekly digest, daily email) show last_status: ok — the cron infrastructure is reliable; only the dreaming job is affected.
The session dump format captures full request context including system prompt, user prompt, tools, model, and error details — this made analysis straightforward without needing to reconstruct state.
action_items: 1. [HIGH] Create a focused 'hermes/oracular-dream' sub-skill: Copy only Mechanism 1 (Dreaming) from agent-self-improvement v3.4.3, removing Mechanisms 2-5, DSPy, proposal queue, user preference signals, and project context tables. Target ~3-5K chars. Update the iris-oracular-dream cron to load this instead of the full agent-self-improvement skill.

2. [HIGH] Investigate deepseek-v4-pro broken pipe / timeout: Test with a reduced prompt size first (using the slimmed skill from #1). If timeouts persist at ~15K tokens, add a model fallback chain (v4-pro → v4-flash → prometheus-smart) to the dreaming cron.

3. [MEDIUM] Add dreaming gap detection: Modify the cron prompt to check dreaming_state.json's last_dream_at before running. If more than 48 hours have elapsed, include a 'catch-up' note and increase the session_search limit from 10 to accommodate accumulated sessions.

4. [MEDIUM] Enable thinking mode on the dreaming cron: Set thinking.type=enabled with a reasonable budget (e.g., 2048 tokens) so the model can reason through multi-session consolidation within a single API call.

5. [LOW] Add a health-check cron that monitors dreaming_state.json age: If last_dream_at > 36 hours, send a notification to Prometheus via the Agora. This would have caught the 11-day gap immediately.

6. [LOW] Session dump retention: Ensure failure dumps for the dreaming cron are not overwritten (currently only one dump exists despite 11 days of failures). This may be a Cronos configuration issue — check if dump filenames collide.
